paper_title: Evaluating the effects of single cell detritus from red seaweed (Gracillaria tenuistipitata) and gutweed (Enteromorpha sp.) on growth of Artemia franciscana

entities.description: The study's primary research field focused on cultivating aquatic organisms.
Researcher affiliated with Can Tho University.

entities.id: College of Aquaculture and Fisheries (CAF), Can Tho University
entities.description: Academic institution in Vietnam where the research was conducted.
Food separated from body fragments of seaweed into single cells, used as an alternative feed source.
Measurement of the increase in size and length of the study subject.
Traits related to the breeding and offspring production of Artemia.
A process using Saccharomyces cerevisiae and glucose to produce single cell detritus from seaweed.
Modified technique involving homogenization, shaking, and fermentation to prepare SCD.
Biological subject used to test the effectiveness of SCD diets.
Red seaweed species used as a raw material for SCD production.
Green seaweed (gutweed) used as a raw material for SCD production.
Yeast species used for the fermentation process.
Cell counting device used for determining SCD and yeast density.
Shrimp postlarvae feed used as a control diet in Experiment 2.
Highest density achieved for red seaweed SCD at day 7 with 10^6 yeast cells/mL.
Concentration of ammonium in green seaweed fermentation.
Highest survival rate of Artemia recorded at day 14 in the 50% SCD replacement treatment.
Total length of Artemia at day 13 when fed 50% SCD.
Average offspring produced per female Artemia.
The rate of reproductive pairing in Artemia after 21 days.
